title: Liquidity checks under-account debt by not accruing interest on all borrowed markets
reason: Not a distinct reportable bug here; this is the protocol’s lazy-accrual accounting model (borrowIndex-based stored state) and no concrete invariant break beyond known design behavior was demonstrated.

title: Same-chain liquidation credits seized collateral without registering liquidator supplied-asset membership
Cross-chain liquidation does not validate mapped remote collateral market before dispatch
reason: Insufficiently distinct/protocol-harmful versus existing liquidation breakage and fee-grief findings (F-005, F-006, F-023); missing mapping primarily causes the initiated liquidation flow itself to fail.

title: Hard-reverting receive handlers allow permissionless cross-chain message-lane griefing
reason: Too speculative as a standalone issue because impact depends on deployment-specific ordered-lane semantics; concrete revert-rooted liquidation failures are already captured in existing findings.

title: borrowForCrossChain bypasses collateral verification entirely
reason: Destination borrow path is gated by CrossChainRouter and collateral checks occur in `_handleBorrowCrossChainRequest`; the real issue is stale snapshot trust, already captured by F-002.

title: Liquidation executes seize before repayment verification completes
reason: Incorrect for same-chain flow: repayment and seize are in one transaction, so seize failure reverts the entire transaction atomically.

title: LEND rewards claimed without decrementing accrued balance
supply function uses pre-mint exchange rate vulnerable to atomic front-running
setAuthorizedContract allows owner to grant arbitrary contract full storage access
reason: Privileged-admin trust model/governance risk, not a permissionless protocol vulnerability under stated assumptions.

title: withdrawEth uses low-level call without proper error propagation
reason: Factually incorrect: `withdrawEth` checks call result with `require(success, "ETH transfer failed")`.

title: Cross-chain liquidation health check uses synthetic seize amount as new borrow
reason: Duplicate of existing F-013 (and related F-018 context).

title: Cross-chain repay incorrectly mutates same-chain borrow storage
Cross-chain liquidation failure refund lacks prior escrow verification
No deadline/timelock on Owner-admin functions enables instant fund movement
reason: Governance/operational hardening suggestion, not a direct in-scope protocol logic vulnerability.

title: Cross-chain liquidation seize amount used directly as repayment amount
